axis: vocal_hexa-scale push (4800 .push() per call) — wire dirty flag, audit env_set, break O(N²) walk
status: COMPLETE — vocal_hexa pattern x5 = 516ms (~104ms/call FLAT), down from >90s timeout
trigger: Per omega_interp_1_partial.json: __hexa_arena_slot_dirty declared but inert; vocal_hexa hung >90s on N=4800.

setter.behavior: Sets __hexa_arena_slot_dirty = 1 on every host-array element write. Covers `array_store[idx] = items.push(item)` from array_push_inplace, plus map_store / struct_store assignments. Cheap single int store; no branch on container identity.

checker.behavior: Dual gate: walks array_store / map_store / struct_store ONLY when (__hexa_arena_slot_dirty AND !hexa_arena_frame_clean()). The arena watermark check (cur block + used unchanged from scope_push) ensures walks fire only when arena allocation could have introduced arena-resident Vals into a slot's items.
extras: ω-interp-2 incremental walk: instead of re-iterating ALL items (O(N) per push → O(N²) per call), walks only items[water[idx] .. len) using a parallel int array __hexa_array_water (grown on demand). Updates water := len after each walk. Earlier items were heapified on prior fn returns and are heap-resident.

ω-interp-2 scalar-VS fast path in TAG_VALSTRUCT non-arena branch: if all 8 polymorphic fields have scalar tags (TAG_INT/FLOAT/BOOL/VOID), skip the malloc + 8 recursive heapify calls. Critical for cached val_int items in numeric push loops.
ω-interp-2 TAG_VALSTRUCT entry fast path: if val is heap (from_arena=0) AND hexa_arena_frame_clean(), return immediately.

reset.behavior: Clears __hexa_arena_slot_dirty = 0 + snapshots arena watermark (__hexa_arena_frame_block, __hexa_arena_frame_used). Each fn-enter starts clean; subsequent writes / arena allocs re-set.

dirty_flag_wiring.visibility_change: Removed `static` qualifier on __hexa_arena_slot_dirty so the setter (defined above the original declaration in the same TU) can reference it via extern decl. Added matching `extern int __hexa_arena_slot_dirty;` at both reference sites.

env_set_audit.current_refcount_behavior: env_set in hexa_full.hexa:6749 is symmetric — increfs new value (line 6752) and decrefs old value (line 6792 / 6816). For self-rebind (push fast path returns same arr_val), incref+decref on the SAME idx nets to zero change. Refcount stays at 2 between pushes (val_array=1 + env_define=2). array_is_push_sole_owner threshold of 2 fires correctly on every push.
env_set_audit.identified_churn: NONE — env_set self-rebind already correct. The original suspicion (incref new without decref old) was not the bug. The actual bottleneck was hexa_val_heapify's per-push slot walk, NOT env_set refcount logic.
env_set_audit.fix_applied: No env_set change required. Confirmed by source inspection (hexa_full.hexa:6749-6826) that decref-old + incref-new is symmetric.

scaling_pre_fix.note: matches omega-interp-1 partial state — host slot walk is O(N) per push → O(N²) per call

test_push_4800_x5.improvement: ~917x faster on call 0 vs baseline; FLAT cross-call (no leak)

vocal_hexa_pattern_x5.description: Mirrors anima_audio.hexa::vocal_hexa(440, 0.5, 100) math + push pattern (sin/cos/floor + 4800 byte pushes per call)

validation.refcount_audit: Source inspection confirms env_set decref-old + incref-new is symmetric. For self-rebind on push fast path, refcount stays at 2 (val_array=1 + env_define=2 + push self-rebind net-zero). array_is_push_sole_owner check fires every push.
validation.regression_test_suite: test/regression.hexa T23..T34 ALL PASS (including T31/T33 Val arena tests). Random examples (array_sum, bench_fib_K30) match pre-fix behavior. anima_consciousness_step / anima_full_engine errors are pre-existing (verified against pre-fix backup binary).

files_changed: self/runtime.c — added 4 changes: (1) extern decl + dirty=1 SETTER in hexa_array_set ~line 1187; (2) arena watermark snapshot + frame_clean helper near scope_push ~line 2270; (3) high-water-mark sidecar (__hexa_array_water + get/set helpers) before hexa_val_heapify ~line 2425; (4) checker upgrades in hexa_val_heapify TAG_VALSTRUCT path ~lines 2509-2700: VS-entry skip when (heap && frame_clean), scalar-VS fast path in non-arena branch, dual-gate on store walks, incremental items[water..len) walk for array_store. Removed `static` from __hexa_arena_slot_dirty declaration.
no_changes: self/hexa_full.hexa — env_set already correct; array_is_push_sole_owner threshold=2 retained; array_push_inplace @no_arena retained.
Linux ELF / docker — not rebuilt (per session constraint).
rollback: cp build/hexa_interp.real.bak.omega-interp-2 build/hexa_interp.real; git checkout self/runtime.c
remaining_blockers: NONE for vocal_hexa scale. The 516ms total is 16ms over the soft 500ms target but the per-call ~104ms is FLAT (no leak). Anima audio-3 hexa-only cutover is now unblocked. Other organs (breath / pipe / vocal_lf) using the same .push() pattern at similar N benefit equally.
